Policy advocacy for Child Rights

Stand for Equality
  • Mission
  • Vision
  • Goal
  • Objectives

Our Mission

The mission of Child Nepal is to work towards the holistic development, protection, and empowerment of children across the country, with a special focus on those from marginalized, vulnerable, and disadvantaged communities. We are dedicated to creating an inclusive society where every child is valued, heard, and given equal opportunities to grow and thrive. By fostering child-friendly environments through education, healthcare, psychosocial support, and community-based initiatives, we aim to ensure that children’s rights are upheld and respected in every aspect of their lives. Our mission is grounded in the principles of participation, protection, provision, and partnership—ensuring that children not only receive basic needs but also have a strong voice in decisions affecting their present and future. We collaborate with families, schools, communities, and government bodies to build sustainable solutions that truly prioritize the best interests of the child.

Our Vision

Our vision is to see a Nepal where every child lives with dignity, security, and hope—free from exploitation, neglect, violence, and poverty. We imagine a future where children are not only safe and healthy but also active participants in building a more just, inclusive, and compassionate society. In this envisioned future, children have access to quality education, equitable resources, and nurturing environments that support their emotional, physical, and intellectual development. We aspire to be a catalyst for transformative social change, where community values and national policies are aligned with the fundamental rights of the child. Through sustained advocacy, awareness, and action, Child Nepal seeks to help build a country where the wellbeing of children is a top priority and where their dreams are nurtured with care, compassion, and opportunity.

Our Goal

The overarching goal of Child Nepal is to promote child-centered development by ensuring that every child, regardless of their background or circumstances, can realize their full potential in a supportive and safe environment. This includes enhancing the access and quality of education, strengthening child protection mechanisms, reducing child labor and exploitation, and improving the overall wellbeing of children through integrated approaches. By working closely with local communities and stakeholders, we aim to create a sustainable framework that empowers children to become confident, resilient, and socially responsible individuals. Our goal is not only to address immediate needs but also to tackle the root causes of child rights violations by building awareness, accountability, and action at all levels of society—from grassroots to policy-making.

Our Objective

To achieve our mission and goal, Child Nepal is committed to implementing a range of strategic objectives that address both the immediate and long-term needs of children. These objectives include developing and supporting community-based child protection systems, facilitating access to inclusive and quality education, providing psychosocial support and counseling to children in distress, and advocating for child-friendly policies and legislation at the national and local levels. We also aim to strengthen the capacity of families, educators, and community leaders to identify and respond to child rights issues effectively. In addition, we work to raise public awareness on child rights and encourage active participation of children in decision-making processes through child clubs, youth networks, and participatory platforms. By integrating advocacy, service delivery, and capacity-building, our objectives are designed to bring about measurable and lasting change in the lives of children throughout Nepal.

Welcome To Child Nepal (CN)

Initiating a Child and Youth Friendly Culture

Child Nepal (CN) is a non-political, non-religious, non-governmental organization (NGO) dedicated to the advancement and protection of child rights, as well as the holistic development of children in Nepal. Established in 2003 by a collective of social activists, the organization is driven by the aspiration to foster a child-friendly culture across the nation. Grounded in the principles and provisions enshrined within the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child (UNCRC) 1989, and aligned with the Universal Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) – 2030, Child Nepal’s mission and operations are firmly rooted in upholding the fundamental rights and well-being of children. Registered under the Government of Nepal/District Administration Office/Kathmandu (Regd. No. 460/059/060), and affiliated with the Social Welfare Council (SWC No. 14240), Kathmandu.

Our Keymilestones

Year 1997
Formation of a child club

Formation of a Child Club named Shrijana Child Club

Year 2003
Formal Establishment

Establishment of Child Nepal with registration in Government of Nepal.

Year 2005
First Strategic Plan

Institutional Set-up and First Strategic Plan Endorsed

Year 2008
First Community Project

First community based ECD Center established and operated in Sindhupalchok district of Nepal.

Year 2009
Partnership with EU

Partnership established with (EU) for Education Project in Nepal serving 4500 children directly.

Year 2010
Partnership with UNICEF

Partnership established with UNICEF and prepared the child friendly Nepali version of UNCRC with illustrations for the first time in Nepal.

Year 2011
First Shadow Report to the United Nations

Submitted the first shadow report of the Optional Protocol on the Sale of Children, Child Prostitution and Child Pornography (OPSC) from Nepal to the UN Committee on the Rights of the Child.

Year 2012
Representation in United Nations

Represented in the United Nations Committee on the Rights of the Child for the first time.

Year 2013
Longest Painting Campaign

Initiated the campaign- Longest Painting in Nepal on Peace, Child Rights and Youths.

Year 2014
Representation in United Nations

Represented in the United Nations Committee on the Rights of the Child for the second time and participated in the 69th Pre-sessional working group meeting.

Year 2014
National Convener

Responsibility of National Convener of Human Rights National Magna Meet and successful completion of the national event

Year 2015
Earthquake Response

Deadly 7.8 magnitude Earthquake in Nepal. Initiated collaborations with We World Onlus, Freedom Matters, UNICEF, The Salvation Mission, Caritas Germany and other development agencies for rescue, relief and rehabilitation efforts in Nepal serving 4000 children and their families.

Year 2016
Girls Samvad

Established partnership with EU for “Girls Samvad” project in Nepal serving 2300 adolescent girls.

Year 2016
First Assessment on Urban Slums)

Conducted the first ever comprehensive assessment of child poverty in urban slums and squatters in Nepal.

Year 2017
Ratify OP3CRC Coalition Nepal

Official establishment of Ratify OP3CRC Coalition Nepal being the national coordinator of the network.

Year 2018
Global Convention of Rotary

Participation in the Global Convention of Rotary in Hamburg, Germany

Year 2019
Partnership with PE&D, France

Initiated partnership with PE&D, France for ECD programs to serve 1200 children below 8 years targeting urban slums in Kathmandu

Year 2020-2021
Global Covid pandemic.

Global Covid pandemic. Initiated Covid pandemic prevention programs targeting children in Nepal and served 5000 children.

Year 2022
Partnership with KODDI,

Initiated partnership with KODDI, Korea to serve 144 children with disability under eight years in Nepal.

Year 2023
20th Anniversary

Marked the 20th Anniversary of the establishment of Child Nepal.

Year 2024
10th anniversary of OP3CRC

Marked the 10th anniversary of OP3CRC adoption in participation of Ann Skelton, Chair of UN Committee on the Rights of the Child in Nepal.

Year 2024
Partnership with FVF, Spain

Initiated partnership with FVF, Spain to serve the children, families and community who are suffering from growing climate crisis in Nepal.

Where We Work

District
  • 1. Kathmandu
  • 2. Sindhupalchowk
  • 3. Bara
  • 4. Parsa
  • 5. Rautahat
  • 6. Ramechap
  • 7. Dolakha
How We Work

Child Nepal is expanding its presence and impact across all seven provinces of the country, with operations reaching the provincial headquarters of Biratnagar, Janakpur, Hetauda, Pokhara, Deukhuri, Surkhet, and Dhangadhi. This strategic outreach allows us to work more closely with local communities, governments, and stakeholders to address critical issues affecting children. Our mission is to ensure that every child in Nepal—regardless of their background or location—has access to protection, education, healthcare, and opportunities for growth.

Message form the Board

Mr. Thakur Khakurel

Chairman
Message From Chairman

Child Nepal (CN) is a non-political, non-religious, non-governmental organization (NGO) dedicated to the advancement and protection of child rights, as well as the holistic development of children in Nepal. Established in 2003 by a collective of social activists, the organization is driven by the aspiration to foster a child-friendly culture across the nation. Grounded in the principles and provisions enshrined within the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child (UNCRC) 1989, and aligned with the Universal Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) – 2030, Child Nepal’s mission and operations are firmly rooted in upholding the fundamental rights and well-being of children.

Mr. Mohan Dangal

Executive Director
Message From Executive Director

Grounded in the principles and provisions enshrined within the United Nations Convention on the Rights of the Child (UNCRC) 1989, and aligned with the Universal Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs) – 2030, Child Nepal’s mission and operations are firmly rooted in upholding the fundamental rights and well-being of children. Registered under the Government of Nepal/District Administration Office/Kathmandu (Regd. No. 460/059/060), and affiliated with the Social Welfare Council (SWC No. 14240), Kathmandu. Through its integrated interventions and initiatives, Child Nepal strives to be a steadfast champion of children’s rights, empowering and safeguarding the most vulnerable segments of the population, and catalyzing holistic, sustainable development for the betterment of Nepal’s future generations.
